topics: Image Perception: MTF of the Visual System, Monochrome Vision Models, Color Vision Model; Image Sampling and Quantization
Image Quantization: Uniform Quantizer; Image Transforms: Two-Dimensional Orthogonal and Unitary Transforms, Properties of Unitary Transforms
Intensity Level Slicing and Bit Extraction; Histogram Processing
Magnification and Interpolation; Image Enhancement in Spatial Domain
Smoothing and Sharpening Operations in Time Domain
Smoothing and Sharpening Frequency Domain Filters
Wiener Filtering; Image Segmentation: Thresholding (Optimal and Adaptive)
Region-Based Methods: Region Growing and Region Merging
Boundary-Based Methods: Edge Detection and Linking, Boundary Tracking
Features: Describing the Contents and Shape of Objects; Classification: Supervised and Unsupervised Learning; Discriminant Analysis
k-Nearest-Neighbor (kNN) Classifier; Unsupervised k-Means Clustering; Deep Learning for Medical Image Analysis
Convolutional Neural Networks; Image Fusion for Hybrid Imaging

Project Implementation Guidelines: Students shall be given awareness and/or guidelines on how to implement and learn this course through a project and are asked to form groups of maximum five members in the first week. Each group should identify a project where different image processing techniques can be used for biomedical image processing applications and perform a literature survey; choose a suitable dataset, proper processing techniques, and suitable machine learning, deep learning, or computer vision algorithms for the application; develop the block diagram, the algorithms, the flowchart, and necessary codes and present them before the jury within the first month after the commencement of classes; implement the necessary algorithms; prepare a report and present before the jury for final evaluation
